message:
fix(indexer): publish semantic manifest after direct cass index --semantic (#203)

The direct `cass index --semantic` path called `build_and_save_index()`
to republish `vector_index/index-<embedder>.fsvi`, but never updated
`vector_index/semantic_manifest.json`. The manifest-backed `cass models
backfill` path does this via `manifest.publish_artifact(...)` inside
`run_backfill_batch`, but the direct path skipped it entirely.

Result: after `cass index --semantic --json` exited successfully and
republished MiniLM vectors, `cass status --json` still reported
`semantic: stale / available: false / current_db_matches: null` even
though semantic search returned correct hits — exactly as reported.

Wire a new `publish_direct_semantic_artifact` helper into the direct
path that:

  - resolves the tier from `embedder_id` (`minilm-384` → Quality,
    `fnv1a-384` → Fast),
  - resolves the model_revision (`hash` for fnv1a-384, otherwise
    ModelManifest::minilm_v2().revision),
  - computes the current `db_fingerprint` via
    `lexical_storage_fingerprint_for_db`,
  - measures the published index file size,
  - loads the manifest with `SemanticManifest::load_or_default`,
  - calls `publish_artifact(...)` with the same `ArtifactRecord`
    shape `run_backfill_batch` writes (schema/chunking versions,
    dimension, doc_count from the embedded message count,
    conversation_count from `count_total_conversations_exact`),
  - calls `refresh_backlog(total_conversations, &db_fingerprint)`
    so backlog/processed counts match the new fingerprint, and
  - persists via `manifest.save(data_dir)`.

If the manifest update fails, the .fsvi file is still published — we
log a warning and continue, which matches the prior behavior of
"index built; status may report stale" rather than failing the whole
indexing run.

Tier classification and model-revision resolution are unit-tested.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
